scan.access_pattern: sequential by 131072-row blocks; classify each block from shipdate zone map into full-pass (max<=cutoff), mixed (min<=cutoff<max), or skip (min>cutoff)

data_structures.aggregation: per-thread uint8_t key_to_slot[65536] initialized to 0xFF plus compact AggState slots[8] and populated_keys[8]; separate block lists for full-pass and mixed shipdate blocks

indexes.purpose: classify each 131072-row block into full-pass, mixed, or skip so full-pass blocks avoid row-level shipdate checks

optimization_notes: The root cause is wasted predicate work in the scan hot loop: the current plan still loads l_shipdate and evaluates a row-level cutoff branch for essentially every row even though selectivity is 98.5939% and the grouping state already fits comfortably in cache. Strategy category C: use the existing shipdate zone map not to prune by min_value, which failed previously, but to classify 131072-row blocks by max/min so blocks with max_value <= cutoff run through a no-predicate fast path that never touches l_shipdate, only mixed blocks perform row checks, and only min_value > cutoff blocks are skipped entirely; keep the cache-resident direct key-to-slot aggregation and parallelize over block morsels to preserve sequential access while reducing per-row work.
